response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web engineer with broad polyglot experience, but the role demands a C++-first developer with industrial automation and cybersecurity domain depth. C++ appears only in a 6-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis project, and it is absent from his skills list entirely. There is no evidence of multi-threaded systems programming, industrial protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), IEC 62443 secure development practices, or any industrial/IoT context — all of which are core to the position rather than merely advantageous.
strengths: Long career with strong software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, code review culture)
Hands-on event-driven and distributed-systems experience via high-throughput micro-services at Dunnhumby and the GISCollective geo-data platform
Familiarity with Node.js, React, and TypeScript aligns with the role's advantageous secondary skills
concerns: C++ is not a current or primary skill — fewer than 12 months of documented C++ work spread across 17 years, with no STL or systems-level depth demonstrated
Zero exposure to industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443-4-1, SDL) or industrial automation protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, DNP 3.0)
No experience in the target industry (automation machinery / industrial IoT), and no evidence of multi-threaded or real-time systems engineering beyond async I/O in a web context
